mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate sustained below 50 basis points eliminates short-term competition for equity capital and suppresses discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% produce negative 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 45% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0% signals severe excess capacity in the labor market that crushes wage income and consumer spending forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns occur more frequently than negative returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in retail sales and industrial production indicates a synchronized collapse in both final demand and production output, amplifying downside earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative month-over-month changes in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment recoveries, month-over-month acceleration in CPI inflation signals reflation of demand and corporate pricing power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when unemployment exceeds 7.0%.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE > 7.0% and CPIAUCSL rises, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a rapid deterioration in construction activity and household formation, triggering cyclical de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% coincide with non-negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with rising industrial production indicates a non-inflationary output gap closure that boosts profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days under accommodative monetary policy.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling UNRATE, rising INDPRO, and FEDFUNDS below 2.0% fails to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in industrial production signals rising manufacturing output and revenue growth, which directly translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when monetary policy remains accommodative.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth above 1% paired with FEDFUNDS below 3.0% produces negative 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 40% of instances.
